label: AppDynamics
description: Connect to AppDynamics to query application performance metrics, events, and health rules from your Cisco APM controller.

form.title: Connect AppDynamics
form.subtitle: Paste your controller URL and API client credentials to start pulling APM data into Anton.

methods.label: API Client credentials
methods.description: Paste your controller URL, API client name, and API client secret from AppDynamics Settings → API Clients. The simplest and most secure path — no OAuth setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via AppDynamics API Client

AppDynamics supports machine-to-machine authentication through API Clients, which issue short-lived OAuth2 access tokens internally. You just paste the credentials — Anton handles the token exchange.

### Steps

1. Log in to your AppDynamics controller (e.g. `https://yourcompany.saas.appdynamics.com`).
2. Click the **gear icon (⚙)** in the top-right corner → **Administration**.
3. In the left sidebar go to **API Clients**.
4. Click **Create** (or pick an existing client you own).
5. Give it a name like `anton-integration` and add the **Default Account** role or a custom role with at least **Account Owner** or the read permissions you need.
6. Under **Roles**, assign `Account Owner` or at minimum the `View Business Transactions`, `View Health Rules`, and `View Metrics` permissions.
7. Click **Generate Secret** — copy the secret immediately (it is shown only once).
8. Note the **Client Name** shown in the list.
9. Paste the **Controller URL**, **Client Name**, and **Client Secret** into the fields below.

### Notes

- The client secret is shown only once at generation time. If you lose it, generate a new one from the same API Clients screen.
- The controller URL should include the protocol and no trailing slash, e.g. `https://yourcompany.saas.appdynamics.com`.
- On-premise controllers use the same path — just substitute your internal hostname.
- Anton uses the client credentials grant to obtain a bearer token scoped to your account.

fields.name: controller_url
fields.label: Controller URL
fields.description: The base URL of your AppDynamics controller. No trailing slash. For SaaS controllers this looks like https://yourcompany.saas.appdynamics.com.

fields.name: account_name
fields.label: Account Name
fields.description: Your AppDynamics account name. For SaaS this is typically the subdomain prefix (e.g. 'yourcompany'). Found in Administration → License → Account.

fields.name: api_client_name
fields.label: API Client Name
fields.description: The Client Name shown in Settings → Administration → API Clients.

fields.name: api_client_secret
fields.label: API Client Secret
fields.description: The secret generated when you created or last rotated the API client. Shown only once — generate a new one from the API Clients screen if lost.

methods.label: Username & Password (Basic Auth)
methods.description: Authenticate with a local AppDynamics controller username and password. Suitable for on-premise controllers when API Clients are not enabled.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Basic Authentication

Use this method only if your on-premise controller does not have API Clients enabled, or you need to use an existing local user account.

### Steps

1. Log in to your AppDynamics controller.
2. Confirm the user account has at least **View** permissions on the applications and metrics you want Anton to access.
3. Note the **Account Name** — for on-premise this is usually `customer1` unless changed during setup (Administration → License → Account).
4. Enter the controller URL (e.g. `https://your-controller:8090`), account name, username, and password in the fields below.

### Notes

- Basic auth transmits credentials on every API call (over HTTPS). API Client credentials (the other method) are preferred because they use short-lived tokens.
- If your controller enforces SSO or SAML, basic auth for local users may be disabled — use API Clients instead.
- On SaaS controllers, username format is typically `user@account-name`.

fields.name: controller_url
fields.label: Controller URL
fields.description: The base URL of your AppDynamics controller including port if non-standard. No trailing slash.

fields.name: account_name
fields.label: Account Name
fields.description: Your AppDynamics account name. For on-premise this is typically 'customer1'. Found in Administration → License → Account.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Local AppDynamics user with at least View permissions on the target applications.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the AppDynamics local user account.
